---
title: "SCRAPI2: Propagating GE and GSI uncertainty in smolt escapement estimation"
subtitle: "Validation against SCOBI::SCRAPI â€” MY2025 steelhead"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{SCRAPI2: GE and GSI uncertainty in smolt escapement estimation}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  eval    = TRUE
)
```

---

# Background

## What SCRAPI does

SCRAPI (Steinhorst & Ackerman) estimates wild smolt escapement past Lower Granite
Dam (LGR) by expanding trap catches to the full passage using a daily sampling
rate. The core formula for daily estimated passage is:

$$\hat{N}_d = \frac{C_d}{R_d \times GE_d}$$

where $C_d$ is the trap count, $R_d$ is the sampling rate (fraction of fish
entering the bypass that are trapped), and $GE_d$ is the guidance efficiency â€”
the fraction of all downstream migrants that actually enter the juvenile bypass
system. The bootstrap resamples both the passage counts and the trap fish to
propagate uncertainty in $R_d$ and the stock/rear composition into the CIs.

## The GE problem

Historically, $GE_d$ is a **fixed point estimate** taken from a NOAA table
(modelled from spill and fish-behaviour studies). Plugging in a fixed $GE$ means
the bootstrap CIs reflect only uncertainty in trap sampling â€” not uncertainty in
GE itself. Under post-ROD operations LGR runs at 80â€“85% spill during peak
migration, where GE is both low and poorly constrained, so a fixed GE can badly
understate the true uncertainty in escapement.

## What SCRAPI2 adds

`SCRAPI2` extends the SCRAPI logic in two ways, used independently or together:

1. **GE draws (`geDraws`)** â€” instead of a single $GE_d$ per day, supply a
   matrix of posterior draws from a Bayesian hierarchical model
   (`fit_ge_model()` / `generate_ge_draws()`). Each bootstrap iteration uses a
   different column (draw), so GE uncertainty flows through into the CIs. The GE
   model can be fit at any stratum resolution (down to weekly), shrink fine
   strata toward coarser parents, and resolve GE to a daily value that tracks
   daily spill â€” see Section 4.

2. **GSI draws (`gsiDraws`)** â€” instead of fixed stock assignments in the trap,
   supply posterior draws from a genetic stock identification model. Each
   bootstrap iteration uses a different draw so genetic uncertainty flows through
   into per-stock CIs. This mirrors the adult-side **EASE** framework: one column
   per posterior sample, one row per fish. `sim_gsi_draws()` generates a
   placeholder in that format from observed assignments while real draws pend.

**Key guarantee (validated below):** when every column of `geDraws` is identical
(or when `geDraws = NULL` and a fixed `GuidanceEfficiency` column is supplied)
and `gsiDraws = NULL`, `SCRAPI2` reproduces `SCOBI::SCRAPI` to within bootstrap
noise. SCRAPI2 changes *what uncertainty is propagated*, not the underlying
estimation arithmetic.

---

# Install and load

```{r install, eval=FALSE}
remotes::install_github("dsmi313/smoltEASE")
```

```{r libraries}
library(smoltEASE)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(readxl)
library(coda)      # gelman.diag for MCMC convergence
```

---

# Data inputs

## Trap data, FPC, spill, strata

```{r data-load}
# PIT tag detection history (full season): GRJ (bypass), GRS (spillway bay 1),
# GOJ (Little Goose bypass) detections are all required by the GE model.
MY25.pit <- read.csv("MY2025_full.csv")

# Daily LGR spill (percent of flow). Used as the psi/p covariate AND, optionally,
# to resolve GE to daily resolution in generate_ge_draws().
spill <- read.csv("spill.csv") %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date, "%m/%d/%Y"))

# Daily LGS (Little Goose) spill: covariate for the GOJ detection probability phi.
# If omitted, LGR spill is reused for phi (not recommended post-ROD).
lgs_spill <- read.csv("lgs_spill.csv") %>%
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date, "%m/%d/%Y"))

# Pull trap data from LGD database
sthd_raw  <- query_lgd_trap("MY2025", "3", "JV")
STHD.trap <- lgr2SCRAPI(sthd_raw, species = "sthd")

# FPC passage data (sampling rates, counts, dates)
sthd_fpc_raw <- read_fpc("2025SMPCollectionsAtLGR_CH1&ST.xlsx", species = "sthd")
sthd_fpc     <- build_fpc(sthd_fpc_raw, STHD.trap,
                          export_csv = "MY2025STHDsmolts_FPCdata.csv")

# QC
check_fpc(sthd_fpc,  "STHD")
check_trap(STHD.trap, "STHD", check_age = TRUE)
check_date_alignment(sthd_fpc, STHD.trap, "STHD")
```

## Strata

Week-to-stratum collapse mapping. Each row maps one ISO week to a stratum
integer; weeks in the same stratum are pooled. This `Week`/`Collapse` table is
reused throughout: as the passage-bootstrap strata for SCRAPI/SCRAPI2, as the
GE-model strata, and (Section 4.7) as the *parent* grouping for nested weekly GE.

```{r strata, R.options=list(width=200)}
strata_raw  <- read_excel("Smolt Strata.xlsx", sheet = "Sheet1",
                          col_names = FALSE)
sthd_start  <- which(strata_raw[[1]] == "WeekNumber")[2]

sthd_strata <- strata_raw[(sthd_start + 1):nrow(strata_raw), c(1, 3)] %>%
  setNames(c("Week", "Collapse")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Week)) %>%
  mutate(Week = as.integer(Week), Collapse = as.integer(Collapse))

# Optional: visualise stock composition by week to inform stratum choice
genstock_by_week(STHD.trap, species = "sthd")
```

## Fixed GE table (NOAA/legacy)

```{r fixed-ge}
ge_sthd <- read_excel("GE 2025.xlsx",
                      sheet = "Wild Steelhead 260406", skip = 23) %>%
  select(DOY = 1, GuidanceEfficiency = 3) %>%
  filter(!is.na(DOY), !is.na(GuidanceEfficiency)) %>%
  mutate(SampleEndDate = as.Date("2024-12-31") + as.integer(DOY)) %>%
  select(SampleEndDate, GuidanceEfficiency)

knitr::kable(head(ge_sthd), caption = "NOAA fixed GE table (first 6 rows)")
```

---

# The Bayesian GE model

## What it estimates

The current model is a **multistate mark-recapture model** (after Hance et al.
2020/2024), not the legacy binomial psi-pool. From PIT-tagged fish detected at
GRJ (bypass), GRS (spillway bay 1) and GOJ (Little Goose bypass) it jointly
estimates, per stratum $s$, three probabilities:

- $\psi_s$ â€” probability a fish passing LGR takes the **juvenile bypass** route.
  **This is guidance efficiency directly:** $GE_s = \psi_s$.
- $p_s$ â€” probability a spillway fish is **detected at the GRS** bay-1 antenna.
- $\phi_s$ â€” probability a fish is **detected at GOJ** downstream.

Each detected fish falls into one of five histories, with cell probabilities
(conditioned on being detected somewhere):

$$
\begin{aligned}
\text{(GRJ, no GOJ)}\ &:\ \psi_s (1-\phi_s) \\
\text{(GRJ, GOJ)}\ &:\ \psi_s \phi_s \\
\text{(GRS, no GOJ)}\ &:\ (1-\psi_s)\, p_s (1-\phi_s) \\
\text{(GRS, GOJ)}\ &:\ (1-\psi_s)\, p_s \phi_s \\
\text{(no LGR, GOJ)}\ &:\ (1-\psi_s)(1-p_s)\phi_s
\end{aligned}
$$

The last cell â€” fish missed by both LGR arrays but recovered at GOJ â€” is what
makes $p_s$ (and hence $\psi_s$) identifiable. The stratum counts of the five
histories follow a multinomial likelihood.

Each parameter is a **hierarchical logistic regression on spill**:

$$
\text{logit}(\psi_s) \sim \mathcal{N}(\alpha + \beta\,\text{spill}^{LGR}_s,\ \sigma_\psi^2),\quad
\text{logit}(p_s) \sim \mathcal{N}(\alpha_p + \beta_p\,\text{spill}^{LGR}_s,\ \sigma_p^2),\quad
\text{logit}(\phi_s) \sim \mathcal{N}(\alpha_\phi + \beta_\phi\,\text{spill}^{LGS}_s,\ \sigma_\phi^2)
$$

Spill is z-score standardised before fitting. Priors are weakly-informative
Student-$t$ for the $\psi$ and $p$ coefficients (with $\beta \le 0$: more spill
cannot *raise* guidance), and **informative Normal priors on the $\phi$
coefficients from McCann et al. (2023) Table 8.3** LGS steelhead. The
hierarchical structure means strata may be defined as finely as weeks: sparse
weeks are partially pooled toward the spill regression.

## The JAGS model

This is the single-level model `fit_ge_model()` builds internally (the nested
variant of Section 4.7 replaces the `logit_psi` line with a parent-group mean):

```
model {
  for (s in 1:S) {
    logit_psi[s] ~ dnorm(alpha     + beta     * lgr_spill_std[s], tau_psi)
    logit_p[s]   ~ dnorm(alpha_p   + beta_p   * lgr_spill_std[s], tau_p)
    logit_phi[s] ~ dnorm(alpha_phi + beta_phi * lgs_spill_std[s], tau_phi)
    psi[s] <- ilogit(logit_psi[s])     # psi == GE
    p[s]   <- ilogit(logit_p[s])
    phi[s] <- ilogit(logit_phi[s])

    pi_raw[s,1] <- psi[s]*(1-phi[s])                # GRJ, no GOJ
    pi_raw[s,2] <- psi[s]*phi[s]                     # GRJ, GOJ
    pi_raw[s,3] <- (1-psi[s])*p[s]*(1-phi[s])        # GRS, no GOJ
    pi_raw[s,4] <- (1-psi[s])*p[s]*phi[s]            # GRS, GOJ
    pi_raw[s,5] <- (1-psi[s])*(1-p[s])*phi[s]        # no LGR, GOJ
    pi_obs[s,] <- pi_raw[s,] / sum(pi_raw[s,])       # condition on detection
  }
  # multinomial only for strata with detection-history data
  for (j in 1:S_lik) n[lik_idx[j],1:5] ~ dmulti(pi_obs[lik_idx[j],1:5], N_seen[lik_idx[j]])

  alpha ~ dt(0, pow(2,-2), 7);  beta ~ dt(0, pow(1,-2), 7) T(,0);  sigma_psi ~ dunif(0.05,3)
  alpha_p ~ dt(0, pow(2,-2), 7); beta_p ~ dt(0, pow(1,-2), 7);     sigma_p   ~ dunif(0.05,3)
  alpha_phi ~ dnorm(-2.60, pow(0.30,-2))           # McCann et al. 2023, Tab 8.3
  beta_phi  ~ dnorm(-1.78, pow(0.25,-2)) T(,0)
  sigma_phi ~ dunif(0.05,1)
}
```

Strata with no detection-history fish contribute no likelihood term but still
get a regression-based $\psi$; their draws are completed in
`generate_ge_draws()`.

## Prepare the stratum-level GE data

```{r ge-prep}
dat_up  <- prep_pit_data(MY25.pit, species = "Steelhead", mig_year = 2025)

ge_data <- prep_ge_data(
  dat_up,
  strat_assign   = sthd_strata,
  spill_data     = spill,
  lgs_spill_data = lgs_spill,
  species        = "sthd",
  goj_site       = "GOJ"
)

# Stratum-level inputs: detection-history pools and spill covariates
ge_data %>%
  select(stratum, spill_LGR = spill_val, spill_LGS = lgs_spill_val,
         n_pool, n_GRJ = n_GRJ_obs, n_GRS = n_GRS_obs,
         n_h1, n_h2, n_h3, n_h4, n_h5) %>%
  knitr::kable(caption = "Stratum-level inputs to the multistate GE model")
```

## Fit the model and check convergence

```{r ge-fit}
ge_fit <- fit_ge_model(ge_data, seed = 123)

# Gelman-Rubin R-hat on the GE parameters (psi == GE) and the regression scalars.
# All should be < 1.05.
psi_cols <- grep("^psi\\[|^alpha$|^beta$|^sigma_psi$",
                 coda::varnames(ge_fit$samples))
gelman.diag(ge_fit$samples[, psi_cols], autoburnin = FALSE, multivariate = FALSE)
```

## Stratum GE (model posterior) vs NOAA fixed GE

Because $GE_s = \psi_s$, the stratum-level model GE is just the $\psi$ posterior.
We summarise it directly and compare to the NOAA fixed table aggregated to the
same strata.

```{r ge-compare-table}
noaa_by_collapse <- ge_sthd %>%
  mutate(Week = isoweek(SampleEndDate)) %>%
  inner_join(sthd_strata, by = "Week") %>%
  group_by(Collapse) %>%
  summarise(NOAA_GE = round(mean(GuidanceEfficiency, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
            .groups = "drop")

psi_mat <- as.matrix(ge_fit$samples)[, grep("^psi\\[",
             coda::varnames(ge_fit$samples)), drop = FALSE]

ge_by_stratum <- data.frame(
  stratum    = ge_fit$obs_strata$stratum,
  spill_LGR  = round(ge_fit$obs_strata$spill_val, 1),
  Model_GE   = round(colMeans(psi_mat), 3),
  Model_lo   = round(apply(psi_mat, 2, quantile, 0.05), 3),
  Model_hi   = round(apply(psi_mat, 2, quantile, 0.95), 3)
) %>%
  left_join(noaa_by_collapse, by = c("stratum" = "Collapse"))

knitr::kable(
  ge_by_stratum,
  col.names = c("Stratum", "Spill (%)", "Model GE", "5th %ile", "95th %ile",
                "NOAA fixed GE"),
  caption   = "Stratum GE: multistate model posterior (90% CI) vs NOAA fixed"
)
```

```{r ge-compare-plot}
ge_by_stratum %>%
  pivot_longer(c(Model_GE, NOAA_GE), names_to = "source", values_to = "GE") %>%
  ggplot(aes(spill_LGR, GE, colour = source, label = stratum)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = source), linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_text(nudge_y = 0.01, show.legend = FALSE, size = 3) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(Model_GE = "#d73027", NOAA_GE = "#4dac26"),
                      labels = c(Model_GE = "Model GE (psi)",
                                 NOAA_GE  = "NOAA fixed GE")) +
  labs(title = "GE by stratum: multistate model vs NOAA",
       x = "Mean LGR spill (%)", y = "Guidance efficiency", colour = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

## Generate GE draws for SCRAPI2

Each `boot_*` column is one posterior draw of daily GE. `SCRAPI2` consumes one
column per bootstrap iteration â€” the same mechanism used for GSI draws.

```{r ge-draws}
B <- 5000

ge_draws_sthd <- generate_ge_draws(
  ge_fit       = ge_fit,
  strat_assign = sthd_strata,
  pass_dates   = sthd_fpc$SampleEndDate,
  B            = B
)

# Sanity checks
stopifnot(nrow(ge_draws_sthd) == nrow(sthd_fpc))
stopifnot(all(unlist(ge_draws_sthd[, -1]) >= 0))
stopifnot(all(unlist(ge_draws_sthd[, -1]) <= 1))

cat("Posterior mean GE by day:\n")
summary(rowMeans(ge_draws_sthd[, -1]))
```

## Finer resolution: weekly strata, nested shrinkage, daily spill

The same three functions support a finer, more defensible GE without changing
`SCRAPI2` at all. None of these is required; all are opt-in.

- **Weekly strata** â€” pass a `strat_assign` with one stratum per week
  (`Collapse = Week`). The hierarchical model keeps sparse weeks estimable.
- **Nested shrinkage** â€” pass your coarse `Week`/`Collapse` table as
  `parent_strata`. Each week's $\psi$ then shrinks toward its parent
  collapse-stratum's mean, so a week with little PIT data borrows strength from
  its stratum rather than producing a high-leverage near-zero GE. Fully
  data-driven; no floor.
- **Daily spill** â€” pass `daily_spill` to `generate_ge_draws()`. GE is then
  resolved per day as $\text{logit}^{-1}(\alpha + \beta\,\text{spill}^{LGR}_d +
  \varepsilon_{s})$, holding each draw's fitted stratum deviation
  $\varepsilon_s$ fixed while spill varies daily. Days share $(\alpha,\beta,
  \varepsilon)$ within a draw, so daily GE is correlated through the
  coefficients (no spurious independent daily noise).

```{r ge-weekly-nested-daily, eval=FALSE}
# One stratum per week
sthd_weekly <- transform(sthd_strata, Collapse = Week)

ge_data_wk <- prep_ge_data(
  dat_up, strat_assign = sthd_weekly, spill_data = spill,
  lgs_spill_data = lgs_spill, species = "sthd", goj_site = "GOJ",
  parent_strata = sthd_strata          # weeks nested in collapse strata
)
ge_fit_wk <- fit_ge_model(ge_data_wk, seed = 123)   # nested model auto-detected

ge_draws_daily <- generate_ge_draws(
  ge_fit_wk, strat_assign = sthd_weekly,
  pass_dates = sthd_fpc$SampleEndDate, B = B,
  daily_spill = spill                  # daily-resolved GE
)
# ge_draws_daily can be passed to SCRAPI2 exactly like ge_draws_sthd below.
```

---

# Three-way comparison

We run three workflows on the same MY2025 steelhead data and compare the wild
smolt estimates and CIs.

| Workflow | GE source | Tool |
|---|---|---|
| **A â€” Legacy** | NOAA fixed table | `SCOBI::SCRAPI` |
| **B â€” SCRAPI2 fixed GE** | Same NOAA table, no uncertainty | `SCRAPI2` |
| **C â€” SCRAPI2 model GE** | Bayesian posterior draws | `SCRAPI2` |

Paths A and B should produce near-identical point estimates and CI widths â€” this
is the validation that `SCRAPI2` has not changed the SCRAPI estimation logic.
Path C should have **wider** CIs because GE uncertainty is now propagated.

## Helper: add Collapse column for legacy SCOBI::SCRAPI

`SCOBI::SCRAPI` requires the `Collapse` column pre-baked into both data frames.
`SCRAPI2` handles this internally via its `strata =` argument.

```{r add-collapse-helper}
add_collapse <- function(df, date_col, strata_tbl) {
  df$.d          <- as.Date(df[[date_col]])
  df$Week        <- isoweek(df$.d)
  df             <- left_join(df, strata_tbl, by = "Week")
  df[[date_col]] <- format(df$.d, "%Y-%m-%d")
  df$.d          <- NULL
  df
}
```

## Path A â€” Legacy `SCOBI::SCRAPI`

```{r path-a-legacy}
STHD.trap_legacy <- add_collapse(STHD.trap, "CollectionDate", sthd_strata)

sthd_fpc_legacy  <- sthd_fpc %>%
  left_join(ge_sthd, by = "SampleEndDate") %>%
  add_collapse("SampleEndDate", sthd_strata)

stopifnot(!anyNA(STHD.trap_legacy$Collapse))
stopifnot(!anyNA(sthd_fpc_legacy$Collapse))

SCOBI::SCRAPI(
  smoltData   = STHD.trap_legacy,
  passageData = sthd_fpc_legacy,
  Primary     = "GenStock",
  dateFormat  = "%Y-%m-%d",
  Run         = "legacy_",
  B           = B
)

# SCOBI::SCRAPI returns NULL and writes output to CSV; read it back.
# Footer rows ("RearSampleSize = ...") are dropped before joining.
legacy_ci <- read.csv("legacy_CIs.csv", skip = 1,
                      header = TRUE, row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE) %>%
  filter(!grepl("SampleSize", rownames(.)))
```

## Path B â€” `SCRAPI2` with fixed GE (validation run)

```{r path-b-fixed-ge}
sthd_fpc_fixed_ge <- left_join(sthd_fpc, ge_sthd, by = "SampleEndDate")

result_fixed <- SCRAPI2(
  smoltData   = STHD.trap,
  passageData = sthd_fpc_fixed_ge,
  strata      = sthd_strata,
  Primary     = "GenStock",
  dateFormat  = "%Y-%m-%d",
  Run         = "fixed_",
  B           = B
)
```

## Path C â€” `SCRAPI2` with Bayesian GE draws

```{r path-c-model-ge}
result_model <- SCRAPI2(
  smoltData   = STHD.trap,
  passageData = sthd_fpc,
  strata      = sthd_strata,
  geDraws     = ge_draws_sthd,
  Primary     = "GenStock",
  dateFormat  = "%Y-%m-%d",
  Run         = "model_",
  B           = B
)
```

---

# Results

## Wild smolt totals

```{r results-table}
ci_df <- function(mat, label) {
  as.data.frame(mat) %>%
    rownames_to_column("Stock") %>%
    mutate(Workflow = label)
}

tbl_legacy <- legacy_ci %>%
  rownames_to_column("Stock") %>%
  mutate(Workflow = "A: SCOBI legacy")

tbl_fixed  <- ci_df(result_fixed$CI, "B: SCRAPI2 fixed GE")
tbl_model  <- ci_df(result_model$CI, "C: SCRAPI2 model GE")

results_all <- bind_rows(tbl_legacy, tbl_fixed, tbl_model)

results_all %>%
  filter(Stock == "WildSmolts") %>%
  mutate(`CI half-width (%)` = round((UCI - LCI) / (2 * Estimate) * 100, 1)) %>%
  select(Workflow, Estimate, LCI, UCI, `CI half-width (%)`) %>%
  knitr::kable(
    caption = "Wild steelhead smolt estimates â€” three workflows",
    format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
  )
```

## Forest plot â€” all stocks

```{r forest-plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
results_all %>%
  filter(Stock != "WildSmolts") %>%
  mutate(Stock = factor(Stock)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Estimate, xmin = LCI, xmax = UCI,
             y = Stock, colour = Workflow)) +
  geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    "A: SCOBI legacy"      = "#555555",
    "B: SCRAPI2 fixed GE"  = "#2166ac",
    "C: SCRAPI2 model GE"  = "#d73027"
  )) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(title  = "Wild steelhead smolts by genetic stock â€” MY2025",
       x = "Estimated abundance", y = NULL, colour = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.text.y     = element_text(hjust = 1),
        plot.margin     = margin(5, 10, 5, 10))
```

## CI width comparison â€” WildSmolts

```{r ci-width-plot, fig.width=6, fig.height=4}
results_all %>%
  filter(Stock == "WildSmolts") %>%
  mutate(half_width = (UCI - LCI) / 2,
         Workflow   = fct_inorder(Workflow)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Workflow, y = half_width, fill = Workflow)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "A: SCOBI legacy"      = "#555555",
    "B: SCRAPI2 fixed GE"  = "#2166ac",
    "C: SCRAPI2 model GE"  = "#d73027"
  )) +
  labs(title = "CI half-width â€” WildSmolts", x = NULL, y = "Half-width (fish)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

The model-GE CI (Path C) should be wider than the fixed-GE CI (Paths A/B). The
difference is the additional uncertainty from not knowing GE exactly.

---

# GSI uncertainty: fixed vs drawn

GE uncertainty (above) widens the **total** wild-smolt CI. GSI uncertainty
instead widens the **per-stock** CIs: it does not change how many wild smolts
there are, only how confidently we split them among genetic stocks. To isolate
the genetic effect we hold GE **fixed** (the NOAA table) and compare:

| Workflow | Stock assignment | Tool |
|---|---|---|
| **Fixed GSI** | observed `GenStock`, treated as known | `SCRAPI2` (`gsiDraws = NULL`) |
| **Drawn GSI** | posterior draws, one per bootstrap | `SCRAPI2` (`gsiDraws = ...`) |

## Build GSI draws

Real genetics-lab draws plug in directly â€” one column per posterior sample, one
row per fish, keyed by `MasterID` (the EASE adult format). While they are
pending, `sim_gsi_draws()` produces a placeholder in that exact layout. The
`"dirichlet"` method perturbs each fish's assignment around its observed stock
(higher `alpha_self` = more genetic confidence; lower = more uncertainty).

```{r gsi-draws}
gsi_sim <- sim_gsi_draws(
  STHD.trap, Primary = "GenStock", fishID = "MasterID",
  B = B, method = "dirichlet", alpha_self = 10, alpha_other = 1, seed = 123
)
dim(gsi_sim)             # n_fish x (B + 1): MasterID + boot_1 .. boot_B
gsi_sim[1:3, 1:4]
```

## Run with drawn GSI (GE held fixed)

```{r gsi-drawn-run}
result_gsi <- SCRAPI2(
  smoltData   = STHD.trap,
  passageData = sthd_fpc_fixed_ge,   # same fixed NOAA GE as Path B
  strata      = sthd_strata,
  gsiDraws    = gsi_sim,
  fishID      = "MasterID",
  Primary     = "GenStock",
  dateFormat  = "%Y-%m-%d",
  Run         = "gsi_",
  B           = B
)
```

`result_fixed` (Path B) is the **fixed-GSI** run â€” identical inputs except it
treats `GenStock` as known. So the only difference between the two is genetic
uncertainty.

## The total is unchanged; per-stock CIs widen

```{r gsi-total-check}
cat(sprintf("WildSmolts total  fixed GSI: %s   drawn GSI: %s\n",
            format(result_fixed$CI["WildSmolts", "Estimate"], big.mark = ","),
            format(result_gsi$CI["WildSmolts",   "Estimate"], big.mark = ",")))
```

```{r gsi-compare-table}
gsi_tab <- bind_rows(
  ci_df(result_fixed$CI, "Fixed"),
  ci_df(result_gsi$CI,   "Drawn")
) %>%
  filter(Stock != "WildSmolts") %>%
  transmute(Stock, Workflow, Estimate = round(Estimate),
            HalfWidth = round((UCI - LCI) / 2)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Workflow, values_from = c(Estimate, HalfWidth)) %>%
  mutate(Width_ratio = round(HalfWidth_Drawn / HalfWidth_Fixed, 2))

knitr::kable(
  gsi_tab,
  caption = paste("Per-stock smolts: fixed vs drawn GSI (GE held fixed).",
                  "Width_ratio > 1 means genetic uncertainty widens the CI."),
  format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
)
```

```{r gsi-forest, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}
bind_rows(ci_df(result_fixed$CI, "Fixed GSI"),
          ci_df(result_gsi$CI,   "Drawn GSI")) %>%
  filter(Stock != "WildSmolts") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Estimate, y = Stock, xmin = LCI, xmax = UCI, colour = Workflow)) +
  geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), size = 0.6) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("Fixed GSI" = "#2166ac",
                                 "Drawn GSI" = "#d73027")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(title  = "Per-stock smolts: fixed vs drawn GSI (GE fixed) â€” MY2025",
       x = "Estimated abundance", y = NULL, colour = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1))
```

The wild-smolt **total** matches between the two runs (genetics only
redistributes composition), while the **per-stock** CIs are wider under drawn
GSI â€” `Width_ratio > 1` quantifies the genetic uncertainty added to each stock.
GE and GSI draws can be supplied together for full uncertainty propagation
(`geDraws = ...` **and** `gsiDraws = ...` in one call).

---

# Control checks (pathway neutrality)

Both the `geDraws` and `gsiDraws` pathways must be **numerically neutral** when
the draws carry no uncertainty â€” this is the strict identity test that SCRAPI2
has not altered the SCRAPI estimation arithmetic.

## Exact-GE draws

Build a `geDraws` matrix in which **every draw equals the fixed NOAA GE**. With
no GE variability and `gsiDraws = NULL`, point estimates must agree to within
bootstrap noise and CI width is essentially identical to the fixed-GE run.

```{r control-exact-ge, results='hold'}
ge0 <- pmin(pmax(as.numeric(sthd_fpc_fixed_ge$GuidanceEfficiency), 1e-6), 1 - 1e-6)

ge_draws_exact <- data.frame(
  SampleEndDate = sthd_fpc_fixed_ge$SampleEndDate,
  # Repeat the same GE B times per row; column names are irrelevant
  # (SCRAPI2 uses positional access, geDraws[, -1]).
  matrix(rep(ge0, B), nrow = length(ge0), ncol = B),
  check.names = FALSE
)

result_exact <- SCRAPI2(
  smoltData   = STHD.trap,
  passageData = sthd_fpc_fixed_ge,
  strata      = sthd_strata,
  geDraws     = ge_draws_exact,
  Primary     = "GenStock",
  dateFormat  = "%Y-%m-%d",
  Run         = "exact_",
  B           = B
)

est_fixed <- result_fixed$CI["WildSmolts", "Estimate"]
est_exact <- result_exact$CI["WildSmolts", "Estimate"]
cat(sprintf("Fixed GE estimate  : %s\n", format(est_fixed, big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Exact-draw estimate: %s\n", format(est_exact, big.mark = ",")))
cat(sprintf("Difference         : %.2f%%\n",
            abs(est_fixed - est_exact) / est_fixed * 100))

stopifnot(abs(est_fixed - est_exact) / est_fixed < 0.01)
```

## Fixed-method GSI draws

`sim_gsi_draws(method = "fixed")` repeats the observed stock in every column, so
it carries no genetic uncertainty. Feeding it to `gsiDraws` must reproduce the
`gsiDraws = NULL` run â€” proving the GSI pathway is neutral when the draws are
degenerate. The wild total must match to within bootstrap noise; per-stock
estimates are shown for inspection.

```{r control-fixed-gsi, results='hold'}
gsi_fixed <- sim_gsi_draws(STHD.trap, Primary = "GenStock", fishID = "MasterID",
                           B = B, method = "fixed")

result_gsi_fixed <- SCRAPI2(
  smoltData   = STHD.trap,
  passageData = sthd_fpc_fixed_ge,
  strata      = sthd_strata,
  gsiDraws    = gsi_fixed,
  fishID      = "MasterID",
  Primary     = "GenStock",
  dateFormat  = "%Y-%m-%d",
  Run         = "gsifix_",
  B           = B
)

gsi_ctrl <- merge(
  data.frame(Stock = rownames(result_fixed$CI),
             null_est = result_fixed$CI[, "Estimate"]),
  data.frame(Stock = rownames(result_gsi_fixed$CI),
             fixed_est = result_gsi_fixed$CI[, "Estimate"]),
  by = "Stock"
)
gsi_ctrl$pct_diff <- round(abs(gsi_ctrl$null_est - gsi_ctrl$fixed_est) /
                             pmax(gsi_ctrl$null_est, 1) * 100, 2)
knitr::kable(gsi_ctrl,
             caption = "Fixed-method GSI control: gsiDraws = NULL vs method = 'fixed'",
             format.args = list(big.mark = ","))

# Wild total must agree to within bootstrap noise
w_null  <- result_fixed$CI["WildSmolts", "Estimate"]
w_fixed <- result_gsi_fixed$CI["WildSmolts", "Estimate"]
cat(sprintf("WildSmolts  null: %s   fixed-draw: %s   diff: %.2f%%\n",
            format(w_null, big.mark = ","), format(w_fixed, big.mark = ","),
            abs(w_null - w_fixed) / w_null * 100))
stopifnot(abs(w_null - w_fixed) / w_null < 0.01)
```

---

# Reviewer checklist

| Check | Expected result |
|---|---|
| `gelman.diag()` R-hat (psi, alpha, beta, sigma_psi; + p/phi, mu_strat if nested) | < 1.05 for all monitored parameters |
| GE draws range | All values in \[0, 1\] |
| Stratum GE vs NOAA | Model GE generally below NOAA at high spill (post-ROD) |
| **Path A vs Path B point estimates** | **Within ~5% (bootstrap noise, same seed) â€” SCRAPI â‰ˇ SCRAPI2** |
| **Exact-GE control check** | **< 1% difference in WildSmolts estimate â€” geDraws pathway is neutral** |
| **Fixed-method GSI control** | **< 1% difference in WildSmolts estimate â€” gsiDraws pathway is neutral** |
| Path C CI half-width vs Path B | Wider or equal â€” never narrower (GE uncertainty) |
| Fixed vs drawn GSI: WildSmolts total | Matches within bootstrap noise (GSI doesn't change the total) |
| Fixed vs drawn GSI: per-stock CIs | Wider under drawn GSI (`Width_ratio` > 1) |
| Weekly + nested fit (if used) | Data-rich weeks unchanged; sparse weeks shrink toward stratum mean |

---
